A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. 11NPSWRD_WQX. Human Activities Scan SEKI16. 11NPSWRD_WQX-SEQU_HAS_SEKI16. National Park Service Water Resources Division. 11NPSWRD_WQX-SEQU_HAS_SEKI16-201604131345SR. Sample-Routine. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 13:45:00. PDT. NPS_HAS. EPA & NPS Human Activities Scan (HAS). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_HAS_SEKI16-201604131345SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 14:40:00. PDT. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_3P_NFKCRK-201505111440SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_HAS_SEKI43-201705300944SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. Sample-Routine. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 10:30:00. PDT. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_3P_1120800-201307011030SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 14:01:00. PDT. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_3P_CRECRK-201907091401SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_HAS_SEKI07-201507221645SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_HAS_SEKI18-201507231332SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_HAS_SEKI08-201507271040SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. notes: 11NPSWRD_WQX. South Fork Kings River Upstream of Muir Rock. 11NPSWRD_WQX-KICA_3P_SFK_MR. National Park Service Water Resources Division. 11NPSWRD_WQX-KICA_3P_SFK_MR-201908051350SR. Sample-Routine. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 13:50:00. PDT. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=KICA_3P_SFK_MR-201908051350SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection.  National Park Service - Water Resources Division and Inventory & Monitoring Program; 2017; Quality Assurance Project Plan for Surface Water Sampling for Pesticides, Pharmaceuticals, Personal Care Products in the National Park Service's Northern Colorado Plateau, Southern Colorado Plateau, Sierra Nevada, Great Lakes, Heartland, Sonoran Desert, and Southern Plains Inventory and Monitoring Networks: Describing Operations for the Year 2017; National Park Service - Water Resources Division and Inventory & Monitoring Program, Fort Collins, CO; 89 pp.. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 13:02:00. PDT. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_3P_CRECRK-201508101302SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=KICA_3P_SFK-HI-201808141228SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. 11NPSWRD_WQX. Human Activities Scan WHIS07. 11NPSWRD_WQX-WHIS_HAS_WHIS07. National Park Service Water Resources Division. 11NPSWRD_WQX-WHIS_HAS_WHIS07-201508191149SR. Sample-Routine. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 11:49:00. PDT. NPS_HAS. EPA & NPS Human Activities Scan (HAS). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=WHIS_HAS_WHIS07-201508191149SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_HAS_SEKI19-201508241200SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. notes: 11NPSWRD_WQX. Merced River at Pohono Bridge near Yosemite, CA. 11NPSWRD_WQX-YOSE_3P_PB. National Park Service Water Resources Division. 11NPSWRD_WQX-YOSE_3P_PB-201509011000SR. Sample-Routine. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 10:00:00. PDT. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=YOSE_3P_PB-201509011000SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 10:54:00. PDT. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=YOSE_3P_TENAYAL-201710031054SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 11:35:00. PDT. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_3P_LDEERCR-201910151135SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. Organization ActivityID=YOSE_3P_EP-201610260850SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. notes: 11NPSWRD_WQX. Kaweah River at Southern Califonia Edison Plant No. 3. 11NPSWRD_WQX-SEQU_3P_KAWRSCE. National Park Service Water Resources Division. 11NPSWRD_WQX-SEQU_3P_KAWRSCE-201411041604SR. Sample-Routine. EPA/NPS-EC PPCPs in Water by Liquid Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry Using EPA 1694, Positive Mode. Water. 16:04:00. PST. NPS_3P. EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants Program (3P). Not Detected. Organization ActivityID=SEQU_3P_KAWRSCE-201411041604SR. NPS_3P_WSAMPLE. 11NPSWRD_WQX. EPA/NPS-EC Sampling for Pesticides, PPCPs & Waste Indicators. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection. A summary for collecting a subsurface water sample used by the EPA/NPS Emerging Contaminants program is as follows. Contact the laboratory before sampling to notify the lab when samples will arrive. At the sampling site, lower an amber glass container to the desired depth (at least 18 inches if possible) with the lid on. Remove the lid under water. Rinse the container two times with sample water and then sample from a similar depth as above. Fill the container completely. Avoid collecting disturbed sediment. Replace the lid and lift the container out of the water. Dry the container thoroughly and label with project name, sample ID, sample date, sampled by, sample time, preservative (ice), grab sample, and analysis requested (pest/PPCP, waste). Repeat the above procedure until the appropriate number of samples have been collected. Use new or clean gloves when collecting each additional sample. Prepare or collect duplicate samples as necessary for quality control. After collection all samples will be immediately placed in a cooler with wet or Blue ice and kept cool until delivered to the laboratory. If samples must be stored temporarily, they should be refrigerated at 4.5�� C or lower to prevent microbial degradation. Samples will be shipped via UPS overnight service to the laboratory. Samples should be shipped to the laboratory as soon as possible after collection and should be received by the laboratory 7 or less days after the time of collection.
